2606.16587.title: Learning Interface Breakup: A Geometry-Conditioned Latent Surrogate for Spray Formation
2606.16587.paper_summary: Designing spray nozzles requires predicting how geometry shapes transient two-phase breakup, but high-fidelity volume-of-fluid (VOF) simulations with adaptive mesh refinement (AMR) are too expensive for iterative design exploration. Standard surrogate models are also challenged by this setting because both the liquid--gas interface and the underlying adaptive discretization evolve across time and geometries. We introduce a geometry-conditioned latent surrogate trained on 797 two-phase nozzle simulations that addresses this by encoding the AMR cell-density field, rather than the full multi-channel flow state, as a compact proxy for where the solver concentrates resolution. From this representation, the model reconstructs transient density evolution and nozzle geometry, and a lightweight second stage recovers the remaining flow variables. On held-out simulations, the method accurately captures key interface dynamics while reducing inference time to 0.045 seconds per trajectory, corresponding to a speed-up of more than $6\times10^4$ relative to Basilisk CFD. These results suggest that AMR refinement structure can serve as a compact and learnable representation for geometry-conditioned surrogate modeling of transient two-phase flows.
2606.16587.paper_summary_zh: 設計噴嘴需要預測幾何形狀如何影響瞬態雙相破裂，但具有自適應網格細化（AMR）的高保真度體積流體（VOF）模擬對於迭代設計探索來說成本過高。標準替代模型在這種情況下也面臨挑戰，因為液體-氣體界面和基礎的自適應離散化隨時間和幾何形狀而變化。我們介紹了一種基於幾何條件的潛在替代模型，該模型在797個雙相噴嘴模擬上進行訓練，通過編碼AMR單元密度場來解決這一問題，而不是完整的多通道流動狀態，作為求解器集中解析度的緊湊代理。基於這一表示，該模型重建瞬態密度演變和噴嘴幾何形狀，並且一個輕量級的第二階段恢復其餘的流動變量。在保留的模擬中，該方法準確捕捉關鍵界面動態，同時將推理時間減少到每條軌跡0.045秒，相當於相對於Basilisk CFD的速度提升超過$6\times10^4$。這些結果表明，AMR細化結構可以作為瞬態雙相流的幾何條件替代建模的緊湊且可學習的表示。
2606.16587.author: Julius H Ramlau et.al.
2606.16587.authors: Julius H Ramlau, Friedrich Hastedt, Tolga Birdal, Ehecatl-Antonio del Río Chanona, Nausheen S Basha, Omar K Matar
